title: Fake Cryptocurrency Trading Platforms Promoted via Messaging Groups. Scammers impersonate stock tip group administrators on messaging apps, luring members to fraudulent crypto asset trading platforms mimicking legitimate exchanges. Targets investors seeking quick returns who join share-trading groups. Extracts funds transferred to fake wallets and steals login credentials for account takeover.

SMS Impersonation of Amazon and YouTube Recruitment. Scammers text victims posing as recruiters for major tech companies offering well-paid remote work. Targets job seekers and unemployed Australians via SMS. Extracts payment for fake training or processing fees, redirects to credential-harvesting pages, deploys mobile malware requesting contact and SMS access.

Scams Prevention Framework: AFCA Appointed as Central Dispute Resolution Body. Federal government formally designates banks, telcos, and digital platforms for mandatory anti-scam obligations from July 1, 2026. Targets all Australians using regulated services. Requires reimbursement of scam losses up to $3,000, with AFCA handling complaints from March 31, 2027.

Fraudulent ASIC Impersonation Letters Targeting Banks. Scammers send official-looking letters with ASIC branding claiming bank staff are under investigation, instructing recipients to transfer funds to 'secure accounts' for asset protection. Targets bank customers through postal mail. Extracts large fund transfers through social engineering exploiting authority of regulator.

AI-Powered Deepfake and Voice Cloning Campaigns Targeting Investors. Scammers use AI-generated deepfake videos of celebrities and politicians in sponsored social media ads promoting cryptocurrency schemes, paired with voice cloning for 'virtual kidnapping' extortion calls. Targets investors and family members with emotional manipulation. Extracts funds through fake investment platforms and emergency payments.

Bank Impersonation Scam Drains Aged-Care Worker's Life Savings. Scammers impersonate bank representatives via phone claiming fraud investigation on victim's account, requesting password and one-time codes to 'secure' funds. Targets vulnerable workers, particularly in aged-care sector. Extracts $33,000+ in minutes through account takeover.

Sydney Man Arrested Over $1.5 Million NDIS Fraud Scheme. Authorities arrest alleged mastermind who diverted public disability support funds through fraudulent participant claims. Targets vulnerable NDIS recipients and their families. Steals government payments through fake service provider invoices and inflated care service claims.

Independent Investigation Uncovers ATO Maladministration; Tax Office Pursued Deceased Taxpayer for Five Years. Ombudsman review finds evidence of systemic procedural failures and embedded misconduct within ATO. Targets low-income and elderly taxpayers through aggressive debt collection. Extracts funds through penalty notices and enforcement action against vulnerable individuals.

MP Pushes to Classify Romance Scams as Financial Abuse; $28M Lost in 2025. Independent MP Zali Steggall advocates legal reclassification as romantic manipulation results in loan take-outs and fund transfers. Targets emotionally vulnerable individuals, elderly, and isolated persons. Extracts savings through fake relationship development over months followed by emergency fund requests.

Fake CAPTCHA Verification Scam Deploying Malware via Command Injection. Phishing pages mimic legitimate CAPTCHA verification asking users to execute Windows keyboard commands (Windows+R, Ctrl+V, Enter) that paste and run hidden malware. Targets Windows users on compromised or spoofed websites. Downloads infostealer malware harvesting passwords and banking credentials.

Loan Fee Scam Targeting Pacific Australia Labour Mobility Seasonal Workers. Scammers pose as migration agents offering assistance with personal loan applications, charging upfront 'processing fees' then accessing victim banking to steal approved loan proceeds. Targets international seasonal workers with limited financial literacy and community networks. Transfers full loan amounts to scammer accounts after fraud approval.

NAB Rewards Points Redemption SMS Scam Harvesting Banking Credentials. Scammers send SMS claiming victim has unredeemed NAB Reward Points expiring soon, directing to fake NAB website requesting Internet Banking login credentials and card PIN. Targets existing and former NAB customers via SMS spoofing. Steals login credentials enabling account takeover and unauthorized transactions.
